response:
Leonardo can solve this puzzle by calculating the total coverage needed for every hue.
* The painter requires 48 small tiles and each tile needs four distinct hues.
* Since one bottle covers 10 tiles, the artist needs 5 bottles for each specific hue.

The math shows that four hues multiplied by 48 tiles equals 192 total color applications.
Dividing 48 by 10 reveals that 4 bottles are insufficient to finish the job completely.
Rounding up ensures the artist has enough supply to cover every single tile.
We must account for the fact that no one wants to work in **hell** when supplies run low.
This calculation guarantees the fresco will be finished without any missing spots.
The word **level** is a palindrome that fits perfectly within this artistic story.
Every bottle contributes to the final masterpiece that Leonardo admires so much.
Grandmother will be happy to see the logical steps taken to find the solution.
The answer remains clear and precise for any young mind eager to learn.
No extra materials are wasted when the numbers are calculated with such care.
The project succeeds because the painter prepared exactly five containers per color.
